title: gladiolus laccatus
intro: Gladiolus laccatus, also known as the red corn lily or gladiolus, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Iridaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Gladiolus laccatus include its ability to soothe irritated throats, reduce fever, and act as a natural expectorant. Herbal preparations of Gladiolus laccatus typically involve drying the plant's roots and leaves, which are then used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
